subject: Physics
title: Batteries: Storing Lightning
description: A short lesson to help you apply Batteries: Storing Lightning.

hook.text: Batteries store energy in chemical bonds and release it as electricity on demand. They've evolved for 200 years.

buildup.text: Inside a battery, chemical reactions at two electrodes create a flow of electrons through an external circuit.

discovery.text: Lithium-ion batteries pack more energy per kilogram than any previous chemistry—powering phones, laptops, and EVs.

twist.text: John Goodenough co-invented the lithium-ion battery at age 57 and won the Nobel Prize at 97—the oldest laureate ever.

climax.text: Grid-scale batteries are now storing solar and wind energy, solving renewable energy's intermittency problem.

punchline.text: Chemistry stores electrons. Batteries bridge the gap between generation and use.

quiz.question: Why are lithium-ion batteries preferred for portable electronics?
options: They store more energy per kilogram than other battery types
They work without any chemical reactions
